subtitle: Egypt, Plagues, and the Red Sea
description: God hears the cry of a people in chains and comes down. Exodus Part 1 follows Israel from Egypt through the wilderness — and reveals a God who rescues, not because they deserved it, but because he heard them.

aboutSource.name: The Bible Project
aboutSource.description: The Bible Project creates free animated videos, podcasts, and educational resources that explore the Bible as a unified story leading to Jesus.

days.title: Exodus, Part 1
days.subtitle: Rescue from Slavery

reflection.prompt: From everything in this video — what stayed with you most?

choices.label: The God who sees and comes down
choices.deepQuestion: Where in your life right now do you most need God to come down into your situation? What would it mean to believe that he already has?

choices.label: Crossing through impossible water
choices.deepQuestion: Is there a Red Sea moment in your life — something you can't go around and don't know how to cross? What would it look like to stop, stand still, and let God fight for you?

choices.label: A name that means I Am
choices.deepQuestion: God didn't give Moses a plan — he gave him a presence. Where are you most tempted to need a clear plan from God before you trust him? What would it look like to trust the person instead of the roadmap?

days.abide: God, you heard their cries and you came down. You are the I Am — not I Was, not I Will Be — present tense, here. You didn't give Moses a strategy; you gave him yourself. Today we bring you the places where we feel stuck, overwhelmed, or enslaved. You are the God who rescues. Move in what feels impossible.
